query: Compare the number of metformin vs semaglutide trials per year since 2018.

summary: The chart compares the number of clinical trials for metformin and semaglutide conducted each year since 2018. Notably, metformin trials consistently outnumber semaglutide trials, with the largest number occurring in 2024 at 154 trials, while semaglutide trials peaked in 2025 at 125 (NCT07281196, NCT07156331, NCT06989177, NCT07150975, NCT06914102, NCT05829460). In contrast, according to the data, semaglutide trials showed a projected decrease in 2024 with no trials currently expected that year, while ongoing trials for metformin in 2023 also reached a total of 138 (NCT05580523, NCT06128967, NCT05847244, NCT06252194, NCT05759897, NCT07003191). The trend over the years indicates fluctuating interest in both medications, with metformin sustaining a higher number of active trials.
